behavior_summary: U8P_TORCH::look: When the player examines the torch it shows the short label ('torch') and then suspends/waits for the interaction to complete.

U8P_TORCH::use: Handles using the torch by prompting/receiving a target selection (captured to local1). If the player cancels (local1 == 0x00) the U8P_TORCH::use ends. If the selected target is out of reach, the script barks "can't reach" and aborts. If the selected item matches a specific item type (0x018Bh), is in a particular frame (0x13) and a particular status bit (0x08) is clear, the script calls FREE::changeItemType with the target's coordinates to change that item to type/frame 0x010Bh (inferred to perform a transformation like lighting/changing the torch).
notable_control_flow: U8P_TORCH::look: simple display + suspend — no branching.
U8P_TORCH::use: suspend to capture a menu/item choice into local1, then: return early if local1 == 0x00 (cancel).
U8P_TORCH::use: call Item::canReach; if unreachable, bark "can't reach" and stop further action.

U8P_TORCH::use: conditional chain requiring Item::getType == 0x018Bh, Item::getFrame == 0x13, and (Item::getStatus & 0x08) == 0 before calling FREE::changeItemType with the target's X/Y/Z and new type/frame 0x010Bh.
open_questions: Are the numeric constants (0x018Bh, 0x13, 0x08, 0x010Bh, 0x0080h) standard engine type/frame/status/range encodings or object-specific values? (inferred; confidence: medium)
Does FREE::changeItemType represent 'lighting' the torch or replacing a held/placed torch with a lit variant? (likely/inferred; confidence: low)
What actor/context check is performed with the pid <=> process target() comparison seen in raw nodes — does it gate which process can perform the U8P_TORCH::use()? (inferred; confidence: low)
Does the U8P_TORCH::look() suspend allow subsequent menu-driven interactions (e.g., leading into U8P_TORCH::use()) or simply pause to let the engine continue? (inferred; confidence: medium)

main_function.name: U8P_TORCH::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short inspect text for the torch when the player examines it; present the player-facing label and then suspend/end the interaction.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the player's U8P_TORCH::look/examine action on the torch (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_TORCH::look
function_map.role: Provide object 'U8P_TORCH::look' description and suspend for interaction

function_map.name: U8P_TORCH::use
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_TORCH::use' action: accept a target selection, validate reach, and potentially transform the selected item

external_calls.purpose: Display the short description/label 'torch' to the player (UI message).
Obtain the current process/actor target for a pid comparison to determine context before/after suspend (inferred).
Check whether the selected target (local1) is reachable from the actor; used to decide whether to bark 'can't reach' and abort.
Return the selected item's type id to compare against the expected transformable type (0x018Bh).
Return the selected item's frame/state index to check it equals 0x13 before transformation.
Return status flags for the item; script checks a bitwise condition (& 0x08 == 0) to ensure transformation is allowed.
Obtain X coordinate for the selected item to pass to FREE::changeItemType.
Obtain Y coordinate for the selected item to pass to FREE::changeItemType.
Obtain Z coordinate for the selected item to pass to FREE::changeItemType.
Transform or replace the selected item at its coordinates with a new type/frame (0x010Bh) when conditions pass; likely performs the visible state change such as lighting/updating the torch item.
